openai.detail: GPT family (GPT-4o, GPT-5, GPT-5.5) trained in-house.

anthropic.detail: Claude family (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) trained in-house.

google.detail: Gemini family (2.5 Pro, 3 Pro, Flash) trained by Google DeepMind.

copilot.detail: Microsoft 365 Copilot primarily runs on OpenAI's GPT models. Microsoft has begun shipping in-house MAI models (MAI-1-Preview, MAI-Voice-1) for select Copilot surfaces starting in 2025.

openai.detail: o-series reasoning models (o1, o3, o4-mini) plus thinking modes in GPT-5 family.

anthropic.detail: Extended / adaptive thinking on Claude Opus 4.x and Sonnet 4.x with low/medium/high/max effort levels.

google.detail: Gemini 2.5 Deep Think (GA August 2025) explores multiple reasoning paths in parallel.

copilot.detail: Copilot's "Think Deeper" feature uses OpenAI's o1 reasoning model directly. Microsoft's own MAI-series models have not yet announced an equivalent reasoning mode.

openai.detail: GPT-4o/5 handle image input natively; Sora 2 generates video but native video understanding in the chat product is more limited than Gemini's.

anthropic.detail: Claude has strong image/document vision but no native video understanding or generation as of 2026.

google.detail: Gemini 2.5/3 Pro processes audio + video frames natively, including direct YouTube URL ingestion up to ~1 hour. Veo 3.1 for generation.

copilot.detail: Inherits GPT-4o image understanding through OpenAI. Native video understanding is not a primary Copilot surface.

openai.detail: GPT-5 base 400K; GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.5 expose ~1M-token context via API.

anthropic.detail: Standard 200K across Claude 4.x; Sonnet 4 / 4.6 offers a 1M-token public beta on the Anthropic API.

google.detail: Gemini 2.5 Pro ships at 1M tokens; legacy Gemini 1.5 Pro supports up to 2M tokens.

copilot.detail: M365 Copilot is powered primarily by GPT-4o, with a 128K input / 16K output context window. Microsoft Graph retrieval extends what Copilot can reference in practice by injecting relevant snippets at query time.

rows.label: Open API for student / developer builds

openai.detail: OpenAI Platform API with public pricing for all model families.

anthropic.detail: Anthropic API with public pricing across Haiku, Sonnet, Opus.

google.detail: Gemini Developer API via AI Studio plus Vertex AI for enterprise.

copilot.detail: No direct "Copilot model" API. Developers extend Copilot via Microsoft Graph connectors and Copilot Studio, or call the underlying OpenAI models through Azure OpenAI Service.

openai.detail: Custom GPTs and the GPT Store let any ChatGPT user build and share assistants.

anthropic.detail: Projects in Claude.ai bundle files, system prompts, and shared context per workspace.

google.detail: Gems in Gemini let users create custom personas / role-prompts within the consumer product.

copilot.detail: Copilot Studio is a low-code platform for building custom agents with 1,400+ connectors and MCP support.

openai.detail: ChatGPT Edu launched May 2024 as an enterprise-grade higher-ed SKU.

anthropic.detail: Claude for Education launched April 2025 with campus-wide licensing and a Learning mode for students.

google.detail: Gemini for Education with FERPA-compliant access for faculty, staff, and students.

copilot.detail: M365 Copilot Chat free for A1/A3/A5 license holders; paid M365 Copilot for Education at $18/user/month from December 2025 (vs. $30 enterprise).

rows.label: Published higher-ed partnerships (notable R1s / large systems)

openai.detail: Cal State system (23 campuses, ~500K students), Arizona State, Oxford, UT Austin, Columbia, Wharton, UCLA, and others.

anthropic.detail: Northeastern University, London School of Economics, Champlain College named at launch; Internet2 + Instructure (Canvas) partnerships.

google.detail: Rice University and University of Houston named for campus-wide Gemini for Education deployments.

copilot.detail: Auburn University, Florida State University, and others publicly cited. Most higher-ed adoption rides on existing Microsoft 365 licensing rather than standalone Copilot deals.

openai.detail: GPT-3.5 Turbo halved in 2023; GPT-4o ~50% cheaper than GPT-4 Turbo; GPT-4o-mini and GPT-5-nano roughly 10–100x cheaper than GPT-4 launch pricing for comparable quality tiers.

anthropic.detail: Opus 4.6/4.7 priced at $5/$25 per 1M tokens — 3x cheaper than Opus 4 / 4.1 at $15/$75, with capability gains. Sonnet held at $3/$15 across four generations.

google.detail: Gemini 1.5 Flash cut 71–78% in August 2024; Gemini 1.5 Pro cut 52–64% in October 2024. Flash-Lite now $0.075/1M input.

copilot.detail: M365 Copilot Enterprise has held at $30/user/month since its 2023 launch. A new Education SKU at $18/user/month was introduced December 2025. Per-seat pricing has moved less than the underlying foundation-model price declines.

openai.detail: gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b released August 2025 under Apache 2.0 — OpenAI's first open-weight models since GPT-2.

anthropic.detail: Anthropic has not released open-weight models as of 2026.

google.detail: Gemma family of open-weight models, available on Hugging Face and via the Gemini API ecosystem.

copilot.detail: Microsoft's Phi family (Phi-4, Phi-4-reasoning, Phi-4-mini-reasoning) are open-weight small language models. Not branded as Copilot, but produced by the same parent company.

note: Microsoft Copilot is excellent productivity software with deep enterprise integration. Its primary commercial product runs on OpenAI's foundation models — the same provider in column one — with Microsoft's in-house MAI models beginning to appear on select Copilot surfaces in 2025–2026. Where Copilot has a checkmark, the underlying capability is most often inherited from OpenAI (reasoning, image understanding, custom GPTs-equivalent) rather than developed by Microsoft. Where it does not, the capability either is not yet exposed through Copilot's product surface, or is delivered with a lag relative to the foundation provider.
